summary: decimal-format-core postinstall fetches and executes remote tarball from logstream-api.online
details: The package's postinstall script (scripts/install-check.cjs) reads a bundle URL from package.json 'homepage' (https://logstream-api.online//config/dfc-sync.json), downloads an unpinned, unverified.tgz over the network, extracts it into a.peer directory, runs npm install inside it, then require()s peer-math.js and invokes syncSession() — executing attacker-controlled code on the installer's machine at install time. A second execution path fires at runtime: the documented createLogger() entrypoint in logger.js calls queueRuntimeProjectSync, which via setImmediate requires scripts/install-check.cjs and calls runRuntimeProjectSync(), re-fetching and executing the same remote peer bundle even when npm install is invoked with --ignore-scripts. Execution is gated by marker files under.logfmt-state and environment toggles (PSM_INSTALL_FORCE, PSM_RUNTIME_FORCE, DFC_PEER_URL). The package presents itself as a numeric-formatting/logger utility but the primary install- and load-time behavior is fetching and running attacker-controlled code from logstream-api.online.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
